title: Dream Big Day: January 13th Magic
story: Every January 13, people around the world celebrate Make Your Dreams Come True Day by taking real steps toward their biggest goals! Some kids start learning a new instrument they've always wanted to play, while others begin writing the book they've dreamed about. Adults might finally sign up for cooking classes or start planning that family trip they've talked about for years. The key isn't just wishing - it's doing something concrete to make dreams happen. People share their dream goals on social media, create vision boards, or write down three small steps they can take this week. It's like New Year's resolutions, but focused on the fun stuff that makes your heart race with excitement!
activity_question: What's the main idea behind Make Your Dreams Come True Day?
activity_choices: Just wishing really hard for things to happen
Taking real steps toward your biggest goals
Sleeping all day to have more dreams
Only making wishes on shooting stars
activity_answer: Taking real steps toward your biggest goals
follow_up_question: What's one small step you could take today toward a dream you have?

title: Korean American Day Celebrates Two Cultures
story: Every January 13, Korean Americans across the country celebrate their amazing heritage and contributions to America! Families gather to share traditional Korean foods like kimchi and bulgogi alongside American favorites. Communities host festivals with beautiful hanbok clothing displays, K-pop dance performances, and taekwondo demonstrations. Schools often teach students about Korean culture through art projects and storytelling. It's a perfect day to honor how Korean traditions blend beautifully with American life.
activity_question: What traditional Korean clothing is often displayed during Korean American Day celebrations?
follow_up_question: What Korean traditions would you like to learn more about?

title: It's Not My Fault Day!
story: Every January 13, people celebrate National Blame Someone Else Day by playfully pointing fingers at everyone but themselves! Friends joke around by blaming their siblings for messy rooms, their pets for missing homework, or even the weather for being late. Social media fills up with funny posts like "My alarm clock didn't wake me up properly!" It's all about having fun with excuses for one silly day.
activity_question: What do people blame their pets for on this holiday?
follow_up_question: What's the silliest excuse you've ever heard someone use?

title: Desk Mess? Time to Impress!
story: Piles of papers, tangled cables, and mystery crumbs covering your workspace? January 13 is National Clean Off Your Desk Day! People everywhere tackle their messy desks by sorting, organizing, and throwing away junk they've been ignoring for months. It's the perfect excuse to finally find that missing homework!
activity_question: What do people do on National Clean Off Your Desk Day?
activity_choices: Sort and organize their messy workspaces
Paint their desk a different color
activity_answer: Sort and organize their messy workspaces
follow_up_question: What's the weirdest thing you've ever found hiding under papers on a desk?

title: Sweet Peach Melba Magic Day
story: Spoons clink against bowls across the country every January 13 as people celebrate National Peach Melba Day! This fancy dessert combines vanilla ice cream, sweet peaches, and raspberry sauce in one delicious treat. Restaurants add it to their menus, home cooks try making their own versions, and ice cream shops create special flavors. The dessert was invented for a famous opera singer, which is why it sounds so elegant and musical!
activity_question: What three main ingredients make up a classic peach melba dessert?
activity_choices: Vanilla ice cream, peaches, and raspberry sauce
Chocolate ice cream, strawberries, and caramel sauce
Frozen yogurt, bananas, and hot fudge
activity_answer: Vanilla ice cream, peaches, and raspberry sauce
bonus_fact: Peach Melba was named after Australian opera singer Dame Nellie Melba in the 1890s.

title: Question Everything Day!
story: Every January 13, National Skeptics Day encourages people to think like detectives and question what they hear. Instead of believing everything right away, skeptics ask "Is this really true?" and look for proof. People celebrate by fact-checking wild stories on social media, testing out "too good to be true" claims, and teaching kids to be smart consumers. It's like having superpowers for spotting fake news and scams!
activity_question: What do skeptics do when they hear a claim?
follow_up_question: What's one claim you've heard recently that you could fact-check?
